post_copy:
An Alaska Native-owned firm just won a $24.96M Navy contract for autonomous stealth boats. They will be built in Oregon.

ReconCraft is Alaska-incorporated, Anchorage-based, and Native-owned. On July 8 the Navy handed it $24,960,408, sole source, for autonomous low-profile vessels, boats that barely break the surface.

The Navy did not compete it. It went sole source through the SBA 8(a) program, built to route federal contracts to Native-owned firms. The boats will be welded in Clackamas, Oregon.

So what is "Alaska" in this boom? An ownership stake and an address, not a shipyard. A real Native win, and most of what Alaska's slice looks like now.

Swipe through how one Anchorage-owned, Oregon-built contract splits Alaska's paper from its steel.

If the firm is Native-owned but the boats are built Outside, does the win count as Alaska's?

#AlaskaBusiness #DefenseContracting #Autonomy
caption: An Alaska Native-owned firm just won a $24.96M Navy contract for autonomous stealth boats. They will be built in Oregon.
first_comment:
Sources for Alaska.Ai Carousel No. 7, the ReconCraft autonomous vessel contract.

1. U.S. Department of War, daily contracts for July 8, 2026. The ReconCraft award, $24,960,408, autonomous low-profile vessels, work in Clackamas Oregon, complete November 2028, not competitively procured under FAR 19.805-1(b)(2), Naval Sea Systems Command, contract N00024-26-C-6324. war.gov/News/Contracts/Contract/Article/4538357/contracts-for-july-8-2026/

2. HigherGov awardee profile, ReconCraft LLC. Alaska incorporation, Anchorage headquarters, Native American and tribally owned, SBA 8(a) certified through December 2, 2026. highergov.com/awardee/reconcraft-llc-10114995/

3. ReconCraft LLC company site. Clackamas Oregon facility, vessels up to 120 feet. reconcraft.com

4. Army Recognition, April 24, 2025. ALPV class figures and the narco-submarine design lineage, about 19.75 meters overall, under 0.61 meters of freeboard, 2,000 to 4,627 kg payload, about 2,200 nautical miles. armyrecognition.com

5. DefenseScoop, March 4, 2026. What the military wants next from autonomous low-profile vessels. defensescoop.com

Full storyboard, report card, and the Alaska AI Docket at alaskaaihq.com
aftercare: Read the draft top to bottom before posting. This was a degraded solo run because the studio subagents hit the account weekly usage limit, so the showrunner did research, copy, and review directly.
Upload carousel.pdf to LinkedIn as a document post and title it The Anchorage Address.
Post the caption, then paste the first comment with sources right away.
Fairness check before posting. The deck credits a real Alaska Native enterprise win and separately names that the boats are built in Oregon. Confirm you are comfortable with that framing.
Per-slide alt text was not auto-generated this run. Add short alt text at upload if you have a minute.

slides.head:
The boat is Alaskan.
The shipyard is not.
slides.body: An Anchorage, Native-owned firm won a $24.96M Navy contract for autonomous stealth vessels. They will be built in Oregon.

slides.head: The Navy did not shop around.
slides.body: AMOUNT $24,960,408 · FOR autonomous low-profile vessels · TO ReconCraft LLC, Anchorage, Alaska · BUILT IN Clackamas, Oregon · DONE BY November 2028 · BOUGHT sole source, no competition · PAID FROM FY2026 innovative technologies (Navy)

slides.kicker: WHAT AN ALPV IS · CLASS FIGURES
slides.head: Built to show almost nothing.
slides.body: FREEBOARD under 0.61 m · LENGTH about 19.75 m (65 ft) · PAYLOAD 2,000 to 4,627 kg · RANGE about 2,200 nautical miles. The hull is borrowed from narco-submarines, tuned to beat radar, sonar, sight and heat.

slides.head: No crew. Not yet a mind.
slides.body: What the Navy wants next: run itself in crowded ports, with GPS jammed and comms down. What it does today: no one aboard, driven by remote control and pre-set waypoints. Autonomous is the plan, not yet the whole reality.

slides.head: A set-aside did the winning.
slides.body: The Navy bought this sole source under the SBA 8(a) program, the federal channel that routes contracts to Native-owned and disadvantaged small firms. Native-owned, tribally owned, 8(a) certified through Dec 2, 2026. The program is doing what it was built to do.

slides.head:
Owned up here.
Welded down there.
slides.body: ANCHORAGE, ALASKA, incorporated here, Native-owned, the address on the contract. CLACKAMAS, OREGON, the shipyard, where the steel is actually cut, hulls up to 120 ft. Two states, one boat, one flag on the contract.

slides.head: Alaska's seat in the robot-boat boom is an ownership stake, not a shipyard.
slides.body: That is a real Alaska Native business win. It is also most of what Alaska's slice looks like right now.

slides.head: An Alaska industry, or just an Alaska address?
slides.body: If a Native-owned firm wins the contract but the boats are built Outside, does the win count as Alaska's? Tell me where you land. Sources in the comments.
